articles.title: Energy Bills Might Go Up in July
articles.summary: Energy bills for homes in the UK could increase by about £332 per year starting in July. This is a prediction from a company called Cornwall Insight, but it might change. The reason is that oil and gas prices are going up because of a war involving the US, Israel, and Iran. A group called Ofgem sets a limit on how much energy companies can charge every three months. This limit is based on prices from March to May. Right now, a typical home pays £1,641 a year, but it might go up to £1,973. The government is thinking about helping people, especially those with less money, if bills rise too much. In the past, they gave support to everyone when prices went high. To save energy, people are being asked to use less.

articles.title: Greg James Rides a Bike to Help People
articles.summary: Greg James, a radio host, rode a special bike for 1,000 kilometers to raise money for Comic Relief, a charity that helps people in need. He started in Weymouth and finished in Edinburgh, riding through England, Wales, and Scotland. Along the way, famous friends like Joe Lycett and Prince William joined him on the bike. People cheered for him and sent messages of support. Greg worked hard and felt emotional, but he was happy to help others. In the end, he raised over £4 million, which is a lot of money for charity. This was his third big challenge for Comic Relief, and he did it to honor his dad, who had a stroke. His parents are very proud of him.

articles.title: A Princess Talks About a Bad Friend
articles.summary: Norway's Crown Princess Mette-Marit spoke on TV about meeting Jeffrey Epstein, a man who did bad things to people. She said she feels tricked and wishes she never met him. Seven weeks ago, people found out she sent many emails to Epstein from 2011 to 2014 and stayed at his house in Florida. She apologized and said she made poor choices by not checking his background. The princess explained she did not know Epstein was a sex offender, even though information about his crimes was available online. She felt angry that his victims have not gotten justice. During the interview, she was with her husband and said Epstein was a private friend, so she did not tell others about him. She mentioned feeling uneasy once at his house but did not say why. This happened because there was pressure from the prime minister and others for her to explain.

articles.title: Labour's Changing Brexit Plan
articles.summary: The Labour Party in the UK is changing its ideas about Brexit, which was when Britain left the European Union. Chancellor Rachel Reeves said Brexit hurt the economy, so Labour wants to work more closely with Europe to help Britain grow. They don't want to rejoin the EU fully, but they aim to make rules more similar to Europe's in areas like food and farming to reduce checks and boost trade. This shift started after Labour lost an election in 2019 and now, with Prime Minister Keir Starmer, they believe a better relationship with Europe can fix economic problems. Some Labour members, like Sadiq Khan, want to go further and rejoin parts of the EU, but the party's main plan is to align rules where it helps Britain.

articles.title: Government Checks States on Health Insurance Rules for Abortion
articles.summary: The Trump administration is looking into 13 states that have rules saying health insurance plans must cover abortion. These states include California, New York, and others. The government says this might break a law called the Weldon Amendment, which protects health care groups that do not want to cover abortion because of their beliefs. The administration sent letters to these states to learn more. Some state leaders, like the governor of New Jersey, disagree and say they will protect their rules. This is part of a big argument between political parties about abortion and health insurance.

articles.title: A New Leader for FEMA: What It Means for Disaster Help
articles.summary: Markwayne Mullin is a person chosen by President Trump to lead the Department of Homeland Security. In a meeting with senators, he shared his ideas for FEMA, which helps people during disasters like hurricanes or floods. Unlike some earlier talk about getting rid of FEMA, Mullin said he wants to keep it and make it work better. He promised to help states and rural areas get money faster and improve how FEMA does its job. This is different from Kristi Noem, who was in charge before and made things uncertain at FEMA with cuts and delays. People who work in disaster response hope Mullin's plans will bring positive changes, but it's not sure yet what he will do if he gets the job. FEMA is important for helping communities when bad things happen, and Mullin's ideas aim to make it more reliable and effective.
